title: Strings, Byte Buffers, Length, Capacity, and Encoding · Failure Case: Encoding Boundaries
summary: An accessible terminal application receives filenames, UTF-8 labels, and binary payloads through APIs that disagree about termination and length. Planned artifact: Add and verify the cmem encoding boundaries increment in length-aware text and byte boundary. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Validate and preserve encoding at system boundaries without splitting multibyte sequences or equating bytes with characters.
Use size-aware copying and formatting with return-value checks and truncation policy.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Encoding Boundaries · Theory Encoding Boundaries
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory native-systems team handles memory case 5324. Produce bounded C evidence to preserve length, capacity, and termination invariants; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Validate and preserve encoding at system boundaries without splitting multibyte sequences or equating bytes with characters.
